title: Identification & Authentication Failures

explain.what: The login endpoint has no protection against automated attacks. An attacker can try thousands of username/password combinations per second until one works.
explain.example: With no rate limiting, a script can attempt 1,000 logins per second. A 6-character lowercase password has 300 million combinations — crackable in under 5 minutes.
explain.impact: Account takeover · credential stuffing across multiple services · complete breach of user data

vulnerable.annotation: ⚠ No rate limiting, no lockout, no delay. An attacker can hammer this endpoint as fast as their network allows.

fixed.annotation: ✅ express-rate-limit blocks the IP after 5 failed attempts in 15 minutes.

demoHint: Use the brute-force simulator to send 20 rapid login attempts. Against vulnerable: all go through. Against fixed: blocked after 5.
